use strict;
use warnings;

local $/;
open my $fh, '<', '/testbed/src/Spectre.Console/Rendering/Segment.cs' or die $!;
my $content = <$fh>;
close $fh;

# Fix Split method - use UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth for codePoint
my $old_split = <<'END';
        var index = 0;
        if (offset > 0)
        {
            var accumulated = 0;
            var textSpan = Text.AsSpan();
            for (var i = 0; i < textSpan.Length;)
            {
                var character = textSpan[i];
                if (char.IsHighSurrogate(character) && i + 1 < textSpan.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(textSpan[i + 1]))
                {
                    var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(character, textSpan[i + 1]);
                    accumulated += Cell.GetCellLength(codePoint);
                    i += 2;
                    index += 2;
                }
                else
                {
                    accumulated += Cell.GetCellLength(character);
                    i++;
                    index++;
                }
                if (accumulated >= offset)
                {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
END

my $new_split = <<'END';
        var index = 0;
        if (offset > 0)
        {
            var accumulated = 0;
            var textSpan = Text.AsSpan();
            for (var i = 0; i < textSpan.Length;)
            {
                var character = textSpan[i];
                if (char.IsHighSurrogate(character) && i + 1 < textSpan.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(textSpan[i + 1]))
                {
                    var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(character, textSpan[i + 1]);
                    accumulated += UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);
                    i += 2;
                    index += 2;
                }
                else
                {
                    accumulated += Cell.GetCellLength(character);
                    i++;
                    index++;
                }
                if (accumulated >= offset)
                {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
END

$content =~ s/\Q$old_split\E/$new_split/ or die "Could not replace Split method";

open $fh, '>', '/testbed/src/Spectre.Console/Rendering/Segment.cs' or die $!;
print $fh $content;
close $fh;
print "Segment.cs Split method fixed again\n";

2026-05-29 14:26:52,205 - INFO - swea-agent-spectreconsole__spectre.console-2082 - 💭 THOUGHT
All 662 tests pass. Now let me also think about edge cases and verify the fix handles them:

1. **Simple emoji outside BMP** (like 💩 U+1F4A9): 
   - Old code: iterates over 2 chars (surrogates), each returns 1, total = 2 ✓
   - New code: `EnumerateRunes()` returns one rune (U+1F4A9), `GetWidth(rune)` returns 2 ✓

2. **ZWJ emoji sequences** (like 🤷‍♀️):
   - Old code: iterates over 5 chars (🤷 = 2 surrogates, ZWJ, ♀, VS16), widths = 1+1+0+1+0 = 3 ✗
   - New code: `EnumerateRunes()` returns 4 runes (🤷 U+1F937, ZWJ U+200D, ♀ U+2640, VS16 U+FE0F). `GetWidth` for each: 2 + 0 + 1 + 0 = 3? Actually, `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(Rune)` for ZWJ returns 0 (it's in `_zeroWidthCf`). For VS16 it also returns 0. So total = 2+0+1+0 = 3.
   
   But the correct terminal width for 🤷‍♀️ is 2! The `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string)` method handles this by skipping ZWJ and the next character, and handling VS16. Our rune-by-rune enumeration doesn't do that.

   Hmm, this is a problem. The `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string)` method has special handling for ZWJ and VS16 sequences, but we bypassed it because it returns -1 for `\n`.

   We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 properly. Let me look at this more carefully.

For the PR description, the title contains:
- 💩 = U+1F4A9 → 2 wide (correct with both old and new)
- 🤷‍♀️ = U+1F937 + ZWJ + U+2640 + VS16 → should be 2, but our new code gives 3
- 🏃‍♂️ = U+1F3C3 + ZWJ + U+2642 + VS16 → should be 2, but our new code gives 3
- 💀 = U+1F480 → 2 wide (correct)
- 🐰 = U+1F430 → 2 wide (correct)
- 🤖 = U+1F916 → 2 wide (correct)

So the issue with ZWJ sequences is still not fully resolved. We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 in our `GetCellLength` method.

Let me look at what `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string)` does and replicate its ZWJ/VS16 handling in our span-based method:

```csharp
public static int GetWidth(string value, Unicode? version = null)
{
    var runes = value.EnumerateRunes().ToArray();
    var end = runes.Length;
    var width = 0;
    var index = 0;
    var lastMeasureRune = default(Rune?);
    while (index < end)
    {
        var rune = runes[index];
        if (rune.Value == '\u200D')  // ZWJ
        {
            index += 2;  // skip ZWJ and next character
            continue;
        }
        if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)  // VS16
        {
            if (version >= Unicode.Version_9_0_0)
            {
                width += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
                lastMeasureRune = null;
            }
            index++;
            continue;
        }
        var wcw = GetWidth(rune, version);
        if (wcw < 0) return wcw;  // returns -1 for control chars!
        if (wcw > 0) lastMeasureRune = rune;
        width += wcw;
        index++;
    }
    return width;
}
```

The problematic part is `if (wcw < 0) return wcw;` which returns -1 for control characters like `\n`. We need to handle `\n` separately.

Let me update `Cell.GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char>)` to incorporate ZWJ/VS16 handling while still treating `\n` correctly:













🎬 ACTION
cd /testbed && cat > src/Spectre.Console/Internal/Cell.cs << 'CELLEOF'
namespace Spectre.Console;

internal static class Cell
{
    private const sbyte Sentinel = -2;

    /// <summary>
    /// UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth documents the width as (-1, 0, 1, 2). We only need space for these values and a sentinel for uninitialized values.
    /// This is only five values in total so we are storing one byte per value. We could store 2 per byte but that would add more logic to the retrieval.
    /// We should add one to char.MaxValue because the total number of characters includes \0 too so there are 65536 valid chars.
    /// </summary>
    private static readonly sbyte[] _runeWidthCache = new sbyte[char.MaxValue + 1];

    static Cell()
    {
#if !NETSTANDARD2_0
        Array.Fill(_runeWidthCache, Sentinel);
#else
        for (var i = 0; i < _runeWidthCache.Length; i++)
        {
            _runeWidthCache[i] = Sentinel;
        }
#endif
    }

    public static int GetCellLength(string text) => GetCellLength(text.AsSpan());

    public static int GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    {
        var sum = 0;
#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
        // We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 sequences properly, similar to
        // UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string), but without the early return
        // for control characters (which returns -1 for \n).
        var runes = text.EnumerateRunes().ToArray();
        var end = runes.Length;
        var index = 0;
        Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;
        while (index < end)
        {
            var rune = runes[index];

            // Zero Width Joiner: skip this and the next character
            if (rune.Value == '\u200D')
            {
                index += 2;
                continue;
            }

            // Variation Selector 16: conditionally add width
            if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)
            {
                sum += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
                lastMeasureRune = null;
                index++;
                continue;
            }

            var wcw = GetCellLength(rune);
            if (wcw > 0)
            {
                lastMeasureRune = rune;
            }

            sum += wcw;
            index++;
        }
#else
        for (var i = 0; i < text.Length; i++)
        {
            var rune = text[i];
            if (char.IsHighSurrogate(rune) && i + 1 < text.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(text[i + 1]))
            {
                // Surrogate pair - calculate the actual code point
                var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(rune, text[i + 1]);
                sum += UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);
                i++; // skip the low surrogate
            }
            else
            {
                sum += GetCellLength(rune);
            }
        }
#endif
        return sum;
    }

#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    public static int GetCellLength(Rune rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune.Value == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        return UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
    }
#endif

    public static int GetCellLength(char rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        // Surrogates should not be measured individually;
        // they are part of a surrogate pair and should be handled
        // via the Rune-based or string-based overloads.
        if (char.IsSurrogate(rune))
        {
            return 0;
        }

        var width = _runeWidthCache[rune];
        if (width == Sentinel)
        {
            width = (sbyte)UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
            _runeWidthCache[rune] = width;
        }

        return width;
    }
}
CELLEOF
echo "Cell.cs updated with ZWJ/VS16 handling"

cd /testbed && cat > src/Spectre.Console/Internal/Cell.cs << 'CELLEOF'
namespace Spectre.Console;

internal static class Cell
{
    private const sbyte Sentinel = -2;

    /// <summary>
    /// UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth documents the width as (-1, 0, 1, 2). We only need space for these values and a sentinel for uninitialized values.
    /// This is only five values in total so we are storing one byte per value. We could store 2 per byte but that would add more logic to the retrieval.
    /// We should add one to char.MaxValue because the total number of characters includes \0 too so there are 65536 valid chars.
    /// </summary>
    private static readonly sbyte[] _runeWidthCache = new sbyte[char.MaxValue + 1];

    static Cell()
    {
#if !NETSTANDARD2_0
        Array.Fill(_runeWidthCache, Sentinel);
#else
        for (var i = 0; i < _runeWidthCache.Length; i++)
        {
            _runeWidthCache[i] = Sentinel;
        }
#endif
    }

    public static int GetCellLength(string text) => GetCellLength(text.AsSpan());

    public static int GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    {
        var sum = 0;
#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
        // We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 sequences properly, similar to
        // UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string), but without the early return
        // for control characters (which returns -1 for \n).
        var runes = new List<Rune>();
        foreach (var r in text.EnumerateRunes())
        {
            runes.Add(r);
        }

        var end = runes.Count;
        var index = 0;
        Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;
        while (index < end)
        {
            var rune = runes[index];

            // Zero Width Joiner: skip this and the next character
            if (rune.Value == '\u200D')
            {
                index += 2;
                continue;
            }

            // Variation Selector 16: conditionally add width
            if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)
            {
                sum += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
                lastMeasureRune = null;
                index++;
                continue;
            }

            var wcw = GetCellLength(rune);
            if (wcw > 0)
            {
                lastMeasureRune = rune;
            }

            sum += wcw;
            index++;
        }
#else
        for (var i = 0; i < text.Length; i++)
        {
            var rune = text[i];
            if (char.IsHighSurrogate(rune) && i + 1 < text.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(text[i + 1]))
            {
                // Surrogate pair - calculate the actual code point
                var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(rune, text[i + 1]);
                sum += UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);
                i++; // skip the low surrogate
            }
            else
            {
                sum += GetCellLength(rune);
            }
        }
#endif
        return sum;
    }

#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    public static int GetCellLength(Rune rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune.Value == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        return UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
    }
#endif

    public static int GetCellLength(char rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        // Surrogates should not be measured individually;
        // they are part of a surrogate pair and should be handled
        // via the Rune-based or string-based overloads.
        if (char.IsSurrogate(rune))
        {
            return 0;
        }

        var width = _runeWidthCache[rune];
        if (width == Sentinel)
        {
            width = (sbyte)UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
            _runeWidthCache[rune] = width;
        }

        return width;
    }
}
CELLEOF

cd /testbed && cat > src/Spectre.Console/Internal/Cell.cs << 'CELLEOF'
namespace Spectre.Console;

internal static class Cell
{
    private const sbyte Sentinel = -2;

    /// <summary>
    /// UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth documents the width as (-1, 0, 1, 2). We only need space for these values and a sentinel for uninitialized values.
    /// This is only five values in total so we are storing one byte per value. We could store 2 per byte but that would add more logic to the retrieval.
    /// We should add one to char.MaxValue because the total number of characters includes \0 too so there are 65536 valid chars.
    /// </summary>
    private static readonly sbyte[] _runeWidthCache = new sbyte[char.MaxValue + 1];

    static Cell()
    {
#if !NETSTANDARD2_0
        Array.Fill(_runeWidthCache, Sentinel);
#else
        for (var i = 0; i < _runeWidthCache.Length; i++)
        {
            _runeWidthCache[i] = Sentinel;
        }
#endif
    }

    public static int GetCellLength(string text) => GetCellLength(text.AsSpan());

    public static int GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    {
#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
        return GetCellLengthRunes(text);
#else
        var sum = 0;
        for (var i = 0; i < text.Length; i++)
        {
            var rune = text[i];
            if (char.IsHighSurrogate(rune) && i + 1 < text.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(text[i + 1]))
            {
                // Surrogate pair - calculate the actual code point
                var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(rune, text[i + 1]);
                sum += UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);
                i++; // skip the low surrogate
            }
            else
            {
                sum += GetCellLength(rune);
            }
        }

        return sum;
#endif
    }

#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    private static int GetCellLengthRunes(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    {
        // We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 sequences properly, similar to
        // UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string), but without the early return
        // for control characters (which returns -1 for \n).

        // First, collect all runes
        var runeList = new List<Rune>();
        foreach (var r in text.EnumerateRunes())
        {
            runeList.Add(r);
        }

        var runes = runeList;
        var end = runes.Count;
        var width = 0;
        var index = 0;
        Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;
        while (index < end)
        {
            var rune = runes[index];

            // Zero Width Joiner: skip this and the next character
            if (rune.Value == '\u200D')
            {
                index += 2;
                continue;
            }

            // Variation Selector 16: conditionally add width
            if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)
            {
                width += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
                lastMeasureRune = null;
                index++;
                continue;
            }

            var wcw = GetCellLength(rune);
            if (wcw > 0)
            {
                lastMeasureRune = rune;
            }

            width += wcw;
            index++;
        }

        return width;
    }
#endif

#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    public static int GetCellLength(Rune rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune.Value == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        return UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
    }
#endif

    public static int GetCellLength(char rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        // Surrogates should not be measured individually;
        // they are part of a surrogate pair and should be handled
        // via the Rune-based or string-based overloads.
        if (char.IsSurrogate(rune))
        {
            return 0;
        }

        var width = _runeWidthCache[rune];
        if (width == Sentinel)
        {
            width = (sbyte)UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
            _runeWidthCache[rune] = width;
        }

        return width;
    }
}
CELLEOF

2026-05-29 14:28:30,800 - INFO - swea-agent-spectreconsole__spectre.console-2082 - 💭 THOUGHT
All 662 tests pass. Now let me verify the fix actually works for the PR scenario. Let me create a quick verification by checking the width calculation for the PR's title string:







🎬 ACTION
cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/verify_fix.cs << 'EOF'
using System;
using System.Text;

class Program
{
    static int GetCellLengthOld(string text)
    {
        var sum = 0;
        foreach (var c in text)
        {
            sum += GetCellLengthOld(c);
        }
        return sum;
    }
    
    static int GetCellLengthOld(char c)
    {
        return Wcwidth.UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(c);
    }
    
    static int GetCellLengthNew(string text)
    {
        var runes = new List<System.Text.Rune>();
        foreach (var r in text.EnumerateRunes())
        {
            runes.Add(r);
        }
        var end = runes.Count;
        var width = 0;
        var index = 0;
        System.Text.Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;
        while (index < end)
        {
            var rune = runes[index];
            if (rune.Value == '\u200D')
            {
                index += 2;
                continue;
            }
            if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)
            {
                width += Wcwidth.Tables.Vs16Table.GetTable(Wcwidth.Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
                lastMeasureRune = null;
                index++;
                continue;
            }
            var wcw = Wcwidth.UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
            if (wcw > 0) lastMeasureRune = rune;
            width += wcw;
            index++;
        }
        return width;
    }
    
    static void Main()
    {
        string title = "Frankie prob poopin' \U0001F4A9 or sum sum idk \U0001F937\u200D\u2640\uFE0F I just love runnin' \U0001F3C3\u200D\u2642\uFE0F n not dyin' \U0001F480 from evil bunny robots \U0001F430\U0001F916 n' shiiii'";
        
        Console.WriteLine($"Title length (chars): {title.Length}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Old width: {GetCellLengthOld(title)}");
        Console.WriteLine($"New width: {GetCellLengthNew(title)}");
        Console.WriteLine();
        
        // Test individual emoji
        string[] emojis = {
            "\U0001F4A9",           // 💩
            "\U0001F937\u200D\u2640\uFE0F",  // 🤷‍♀️
            "\U0001F3C3\u200D\u2642\uFE0F",  // 🏃‍♂️
            "\U0001F480",           // 💀
            "\U0001F430",           // 🐰
            "\U0001F916",           // 🤖
        };
        
        foreach (var emoji in emojis)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Emoji: {emoji} old={GetCellLengthOld(emoji)} new={GetCellLengthNew(emoji)}");
        }
    }
}
EOF

2026-05-29 14:28:59,871 - DEBUG - swea-lm-spectreconsole__spectre.console-2082 - Response: ModelResponse(id='4234404450aa455195fe61ae3d65ad8f', created=1780064914, model='DeepSeek-V4-Pro', object='chat.completion', system_fingerprint=None, choices=[Choices(finish_reason='tool_calls', index=0, message=Message(content='The verification script can\'t be run easily due to project setup issues. But let me reason through the fix:\n\nFor the title string from the PR:\n```\n"Frankie prob poopin\' 💩 or sum sum idk 🤷\u200d♀️ I just love runnin\' 🏃\u200d♂️ n not dyin\' 💀 from evil bunny robots 🐰🤖 n\' shiiii\'"\n```\n\nLet me calculate widths:\n\n**Old method (char-by-char):**\n- 💩 (U+1F4A9): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2\n- 🤷\u200d♀️ (U+1F937 + ZWJ + U+2640 + VS16): 2 + 0 + 1 + 0 = 3\n- 🏃\u200d♂️ (U+1F3C3 + ZWJ + U+2642 + VS16): 2 + 0 + 1 + 0 = 3\n- 💀 (U+1F480): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2\n- 🐰 (U+1F430): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2\n- 🤖 (U+1F916): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2\n\nTotal emoji width: 2 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 14\nRest of text (ASCII): 101 chars\nTotal: 115\n\n**New method (rune-based with ZWJ/VS16 handling):**\n- 💩: 1 rune, width = 2\n- 🤷\u200d♀️: 4 runes (U+1F937, ZWJ, U+2640, VS16), ZWJ skips, VS16 handled:\n  - U+1F937: width 2, lastMeasureRune = this\n  - ZWJ: skip (index += 2)\n  - VS16: check Vs16Table for U+1F937, probably adds 0\n  - Total: 2\n- 🏃\u200d♂️: similar, width = 2\n- 💀: 1 rune, width = 2\n- 🐰: 1 rune, width = 2\n- 🤖: 1 rune, width = 2\n\nTotal emoji width: 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 12\nRest of text: 101\nTotal: 113\n\nSo the new width is 2 less than the old width. This means the table would need 2 fewer cells to align correctly.\n\nThe PR description shows that the table border is broken (right border doesn\'t align). With the old code, the title cell width was calculated as 115, but the actual display width is 113 (because ZWJ sequences are 2 cells wide, not 3). The table uses the calculated width to determine column widths, so the right border would be off by 2 characters.\n\nWith our fix, the calculated width is 113, which matches the actual display width, so the table should render correctly.\n\nNow let me also think about edge cases:\n\n1. **Multiple ZWJ sequences**: e.g., family emoji 👨\u200d👩\u200d👧\u200d👦 (man + ZWJ + woman + ZWJ + girl + ZWJ + boy). Our code skips ZWJ and the next character, so:\n   - Man (U+1F468): width 2\n   - ZWJ: skip, next is woman, skipped\n   - ZWJ: skip, next is girl, skipped\n   - ZWJ: skip, next is boy, skipped\n   - Total: 2 ✓ (correct, the whole family emoji is 2 cells wide)\n\n2. **VS16 without preceding wide char**: e.g., variation selector on a narrow character. Our code checks `lastMeasureRune != null` before processing VS16, so it\'s safe.\n\n3. **Plain ASCII text**: Should be unchanged since each char maps to a single rune with width 1.\n\n4. **CJK characters**: These are in the BMP (mostly) and have width 2. Our rune enumeration handles them correctly.\n\n5. **Newlines in strings**: `GetCellLength(Rune)` special-cases `\\n` to return 1, same as before.\n\nNow, one more thing: I should also check if the `Cell.GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char>)` method is called with text that contains `\\n`. If so, the ZWJ/VS16 handling loop would see `\\n` as a rune and call `GetCellLength(Rune)` which returns 1 (our special case). That\'s correct.\n\nBut wait, there\'s a subtle issue: in my `GetCellLengthRunes` method, I check `if (rune.Value == \'\\u200D\')` and skip by `index += 2`. But I also check `if (rune.Value == \'\\uFE0F\' && lastMeasureRune != null)` and `lastMeasureRune` is from a previous iteration. This logic mirrors the `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string)` method.\n\nHowever, there\'s one difference: in `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string)`, it returns -1 immediately if any character has width < 0. Our code doesn\'t do that - we always return non-negative values because `GetCellLength(Rune)` returns at least 0 (or 1 for `\\n`).\n\nI think the fix is solid. Let me now also verify that we haven\'t introduced any performance regressions. The `GetCellLengthRunes` method allocates a `List<Rune>` for each call. This could be a performance issue for frequently-called code. Let me optimize it to avoid the allocation.\n\nInstead of collecting all runes first, I can iterate through the span using a while loop with `Rune.DecodeFromUtf16`:\n\n\n\n\n\n', role='assistant', tool_calls=[ChatCompletionMessageToolCall(index=0, function=Function(arguments='{"command": "cd /testbed && cat > src/Spectre.Console/Internal/Cell.cs << \'CELLEOF\'\\nnamespace Spectre.Console;\\n\\ninternal static class Cell\\n{\\n    private const sbyte Sentinel = -2;\\n\\n    /// <summary>\\n    /// UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth documents the width as (-1, 0, 1, 2). We only need space for these values and a sentinel for uninitialized values.\\n    /// This is only five values in total so we are storing one byte per value. We could store 2 per byte but that would add more logic to the retrieval.\\n    /// We should add one to char.MaxValue because the total number of characters includes \\\\0 too so there are 65536 valid chars.\\n    /// </summary>\\n    private static readonly sbyte[] _runeWidthCache = new sbyte[char.MaxValue + 1];\\n\\n    static Cell()\\n    {\\n#if !NETSTANDARD2_0\\n        Array.Fill(_runeWidthCache, Sentinel);\\n#else\\n        for (var i = 0; i < _runeWidthCache.Length; i++)\\n        {\\n            _runeWidthCache[i] = Sentinel;\\n        }\\n#endif\\n    }\\n\\n    public static int GetCellLength(string text) => GetCellLength(text.AsSpan());\\n\\n    public static int GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)\\n    {\\n#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER\\n        return GetCellLengthRunes(text);\\n#else\\n        var sum = 0;\\n        for (var i = 0; i < text.Length; i++)\\n        {\\n            var rune = text[i];\\n            if (char.IsHighSurrogate(rune) && i + 1 < text.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(text[i + 1]))\\n            {\\n                // Surrogate pair - calculate the actual code point\\n                var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(rune, text[i + 1]);\\n                sum += UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);\\n                i++; // skip the low surrogate\\n            }\\n            else\\n            {\\n                sum += GetCellLength(rune);\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        return sum;\\n#endif\\n    }\\n\\n#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER\\n    private static int GetCellLengthRunes(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)\\n    {\\n        // We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 sequences properly.\\n        // We iterate through the span, decoding runes as we go,\\n        // and maintain state for ZWJ/VS16 sequences.\\n\\n        var width = 0;\\n        var i = 0;\\n        Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;\\n\\n        while (i < text.Length)\\n        {\\n            System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out var rune, out var charsConsumed);\\n            i += charsConsumed;\\n\\n            // Zero Width Joiner: skip this and the next rune\\n            if (rune.Value == \'\\\\u200D\')\\n            {\\n                // Skip the next rune\\n                if (i < text.Length)\\n                {\\n                    System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out _, out var skipChars);\\n                    i += skipChars;\\n                }\\n\\n                continue;\\n            }\\n\\n            // Variation Selector 16: conditionally add width\\n            if (rune.Value == \'\\\\uFE0F\' && lastMeasureRune != null)\\n            {\\n                width += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);\\n                lastMeasureRune = null;\\n width += wcw;\\n        }\\n\\n        return width;\\n    }\\n#endif\\n\\n#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER\\n    public static int GetCellLength(Rune rune)\\n    {\\n        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails\\n        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)\\n        // calculate the size for new line characters.\\n        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the\\n        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used\\n        // to work with string length and not cell length.\\n        if (rune.Value == \'\\\\n\')\\n        {\\n            return 1;\\n        }\\n\\n        return UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);\\n    }\\n#endif\\n\\n    public static int GetCellLength(char rune)\\n    {\\n        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails\\n        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)\\n        // calculate the size for new line characters.\\n        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the\\n        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used\\n        // to work with string length and not cell length.\\n        if (rune == \'\\\\n\')\\n        {\\n            return 1;\\n        }\\n\\n        // Surrogates should not be measured individually;\\n        // they are part of a surrogate pair and should be handled\\n        // via the 2026-05-29 14:28:59,880 - DEBUG - swea-lm-spectreconsole__spectre.console-2082 - total_tokens_sent=5,850,417, total_tokens_received=18,851, total_cost=0.00, total_api_calls=92
2026-05-29 14:28:59,882 - INFO - swea-agent-spectreconsole__spectre.console-2082 - 💭 THOUGHT
The verification script can't be run easily due to project setup issues. But let me reason through the fix:

For the title string from the PR:
```
"Frankie prob poopin' 💩 or sum sum idk 🤷‍♀️ I just love runnin' 🏃‍♂️ n not dyin' 💀 from evil bunny robots 🐰🤖 n' shiiii'"
```

Let me calculate widths:

**Old method (char-by-char):**
- 💩 (U+1F4A9): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2
- 🤷‍♀️ (U+1F937 + ZWJ + U+2640 + VS16): 2 + 0 + 1 + 0 = 3
- 🏃‍♂️ (U+1F3C3 + ZWJ + U+2642 + VS16): 2 + 0 + 1 + 0 = 3
- 💀 (U+1F480): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2
- 🐰 (U+1F430): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2
- 🤖 (U+1F916): 2 surrogates × 1 = 2

Total emoji width: 2 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 14
Rest of text (ASCII): 101 chars
Total: 115

**New method (rune-based with ZWJ/VS16 handling):**
- 💩: 1 rune, width = 2
- 🤷‍♀️: 4 runes (U+1F937, ZWJ, U+2640, VS16), ZWJ skips, VS16 handled:
  - U+1F937: width 2, lastMeasureRune = this
  - ZWJ: skip (index += 2)
  - VS16: check Vs16Table for U+1F937, probably adds 0
  - Total: 2
- 🏃‍♂️: similar, width = 2
- 💀: 1 rune, width = 2
- 🐰: 1 rune, width = 2
- 🤖: 1 rune, width = 2

Total emoji width: 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 12
Rest of text: 101
Total: 113

So the new width is 2 less than the old width. This means the table would need 2 fewer cells to align correctly.

The PR description shows that the table border is broken (right border doesn't align). With the old code, the title cell width was calculated as 115, but the actual display width is 113 (because ZWJ sequences are 2 cells wide, not 3). The table uses the calculated width to determine column widths, so the right border would be off by 2 characters.

With our fix, the calculated width is 113, which matches the actual display width, so the table should render correctly.

Now let me also think about edge cases:

1. **Multiple ZWJ sequences**: e.g., family emoji 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 (man + ZWJ + woman + ZWJ + girl + ZWJ + boy). Our code skips ZWJ and the next character, so:
   - Man (U+1F468): width 2
   - ZWJ: skip, next is woman, skipped
   - ZWJ: skip, next is girl, skipped
   - ZWJ: skip, next is boy, skipped
   - Total: 2 ✓ (correct, the whole family emoji is 2 cells wide)

2. **VS16 without preceding wide char**: e.g., variation selector on a narrow character. Our code checks `lastMeasureRune != null` before processing VS16, so it's safe.

3. **Plain ASCII text**: Should be unchanged since each char maps to a single rune with width 1.

4. **CJK characters**: These are in the BMP (mostly) and have width 2. Our rune enumeration handles them correctly.

5. **Newlines in strings**: `GetCellLength(Rune)` special-cases `\n` to return 1, same as before.

Now, one more thing: I should also check if the `Cell.GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char>)` method is called with text that contains `\n`. If so, the ZWJ/VS16 handling loop would see `\n` as a rune and call `GetCellLength(Rune)` which returns 1 (our special case). That's correct.

But wait, there's a subtle issue: in my `GetCellLengthRunes` method, I check `if (rune.Value == '\u200D')` and skip by `index += 2`. But I also check `if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)` and `lastMeasureRune` is from a previous iteration. This logic mirrors the `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string)` method.

However, there's one difference: in `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(string)`, it returns -1 immediately if any character has width < 0. Our code doesn't do that - we always return non-negative values because `GetCellLength(Rune)` returns at least 0 (or 1 for `\n`).

I think the fix is solid. Let me now also verify that we haven't introduced any performance regressions. The `GetCellLengthRunes` method allocates a `List<Rune>` for each call. This could be a performance issue for frequently-called code. Let me optimize it to avoid the allocation.

Instead of collecting all runes first, I can iterate through the span using a while loop with `Rune.DecodeFromUtf16`:







🎬 ACTION
cd /testbed && cat > src/Spectre.Console/Internal/Cell.cs << 'CELLEOF'
namespace Spectre.Console;

internal static class Cell
{
    private const sbyte Sentinel = -2;

    /// <summary>
    /// UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth documents the width as (-1, 0, 1, 2). We only need space for these values and a sentinel for uninitialized values.
    /// This is only five values in total so we are storing one byte per value. We could store 2 per byte but that would add more logic to the retrieval.
    /// We should add one to char.MaxValue because the total number of characters includes \0 too so there are 65536 valid chars.
    /// </summary>
    private static readonly sbyte[] _runeWidthCache = new sbyte[char.MaxValue + 1];

    static Cell()
    {
#if !NETSTANDARD2_0
        Array.Fill(_runeWidthCache, Sentinel);
#else
        for (var i = 0; i < _runeWidthCache.Length; i++)
        {
            _runeWidthCache[i] = Sentinel;
        }
#endif
    }

    public static int GetCellLength(string text) => GetCellLength(text.AsSpan());

    public static int GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    {
#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
        return GetCellLengthRunes(text);
#else
        var sum = 0;
        for (var i = 0; i < text.Length; i++)
        {
            var rune = text[i];
            if (char.IsHighSurrogate(rune) && i + 1 < text.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(text[i + 1]))
            {
                // Surrogate pair - calculate the actual code point
                var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(rune, text[i + 1]);
                sum += UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);
                i++; // skip the low surrogate
            }
            else
            {
                sum += GetCellLength(rune);
            }
        }

        return sum;
#endif
    }

#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    private static int GetCellLengthRunes(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    {
        // We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 sequences properly.
        // We iterate through the span, decoding runes as we go,
        // and maintain state for ZWJ/VS16 sequences.

        var width = 0;
        var i = 0;
        Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;

        while (i < text.Length)
        {
            System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out var rune, out var charsConsumed);
            i += charsConsumed;

            // Zero Width Joiner: skip this and the next rune
            if (rune.Value == '\u200D')
            {
                // Skip the next rune
                if (i < text.Length)
                {
                    System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out _, out var skipChars);
                    i += skipChars;
                }

                continue;
            }

            // Variation Selector 16: conditionally add width
            if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)
            {
                width += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
                lastMeasureRune = null;
                continue;
            }

            var wcw = GetCellLength(rune);
            if (wcw > 0)
            {
                lastMeasureRune = rune;
            }

            width += wcw;
        }

        return width;
    }
#endif

#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    public static int GetCellLength(Rune rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune.Value == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        return UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
    }
#endif

    public static int GetCellLength(char rune)
    {
        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
        // calculate the size for new line characters.
        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
        // to work with string length and not cell length.
        if (rune == '\n')
        {
            return 1;
        }

        // Surrogates should not be measured individually;
        // they are part of a surrogate pair and should be handled
        // via the Rune-based or string-based overloads.
        if (char.IsSurrogate(rune))
        {
            return 0;
        }

        var width = _runeWidthCache[rune];
        if (width == Sentinel)
        {
            width = (sbyte)UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
            _runeWidthCache[rune] = width;
        }

        return width;
    }
}
CELLEOF

   424	
   425	    /// <summary>
   426	    /// Truncates the segment to the specified width.
   427	    /// </summary>
   428	    /// <param name="segment">The segment to truncate.</param>
   429	    /// <param name="maxWidth">The maximum width that the segment may occupy.</param>
   430	    /// <returns>A new truncated segment, or <c>null</c>.</returns>
   431	    public static Segment? Truncate(Segment? segment, int maxWidth)
   432	    {
   433	        if (segment is null)
   434	        {
   435	            return null;
   436	        }
   437	
   438	        if (segment.CellCount() <= maxWidth)
   439	        {
   440	            return segment;
   441	        }
   442	
   443	        var builder = new StringBuilder();
   444	        var accumulatedCellWidth = 0;
   445	        var textSpan = segment.Text.AsSpan();
   446	        for (var i = 0; i < textSpan.Length;)
   447	        {
   448	            var character = textSpan[i];
   449	            if (char.IsHighSurrogate(character) && i + 1 < textSpan.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(textSpan[i + 1]))
   450	            {
   451	                var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(character, textSpan[i + 1]);
   452	                var characterWidth = UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);
   453	                if (accumulatedCellWidth + characterWidth > maxWidth)
   454	                {
   455	                    break;
   456	                }
   457	                builder.Append(character);
   458	                builder.Append(textSpan[i + 1]);
   459	                accumulatedCellWidth += characterWidth;
   460	                i += 2;
   461	            }
   462	            else
   463	            {
   464	                var characterWidth = UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(character);
   465	                if (accumulatedCellWidth + characterWidth > maxWidth)
   466	                {
   467	                    break;
   468	                }
   469	                builder.Append(character);
   470	                accumulatedCellWidth += characterWidth;
   471	                i++;
   472	            }
   473	        }
   474	
   475	        if (builder.Length == 0)

2026-05-29 14:31:59,998 - INFO - swea-agent-spectreconsole__spectre.console-2082 - 💭 THOUGHT
Hmm, there's an issue with `Truncate`. It uses `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(character)` for regular chars. But `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(char)` for surrogates returns 1 (it doesn't return 0). But my code handles surrogates separately, which is correct. However, for non-surrogate chars, it uses `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(char)` which could return -1 for control characters. This could be a problem.

But actually, looking at the original code, it also used `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(character)` directly (not `Cell.GetCellLength`). So this behavior is unchanged. Let me not change that for now to minimize risk.

The `SplitSegment` method also uses `UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(ch)` directly for non-surrogate chars, which is consistent with the original behavior.

Now, there's one more thing to consider: the `Cell.GetCellLength(char)` now returns 0 for surrogates. But some code calls `Cell.GetCellLength(char)` directly on chars that might be surrogates. Let me check where `Cell.GetCellLength(char)` is called:

1. In `Cell.GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char>)` for netstandard2.0: we handle surrogates before calling `GetCellLength(char)`, so it's fine.
2. In `Segment.Split`: we handle surrogates before calling `GetCellLength(char)`, so it's fine.
3. In `CharExtensions.GetCellWidth`: this is called by external code. If someone calls `'💩'.GetCellWidth()` (which is a single char, not a valid operation for emoji), it would return 0 now instead of 1. But this is actually more correct - a standalone surrogate shouldn't have a width.

Let me check if `CharExtensions.GetCellWidth` is used anywhere:

     1	namespace Spectre.Console;
     2	
     3	internal static class Cell
     4	{
     5	    private const sbyte Sentinel = -2;
     6	
     7	    /// <summary>
     8	    /// UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth documents the width as (-1, 0, 1, 2). We only need space for these values and a sentinel for uninitialized values.
     9	    /// This is only five values in total so we are storing one byte per value. We could store 2 per byte but that would add more logic to the retrieval.
    10	    /// We should add one to char.MaxValue because the total number of characters includes \0 too so there are 65536 valid chars.
    11	    /// </summary>
    12	    private static readonly sbyte[] _runeWidthCache = new sbyte[char.MaxValue + 1];
    13	
    14	    static Cell()
    15	    {
    16	#if !NETSTANDARD2_0
    17	        Array.Fill(_runeWidthCache, Sentinel);
    18	#else
    19	        for (var i = 0; i < _runeWidthCache.Length; i++)
    20	        {
    21	            _runeWidthCache[i] = Sentinel;
    22	        }
    23	#endif
    24	    }
    25	
    26	    public static int GetCellLength(string text) => GetCellLength(text.AsSpan());
    27	
    28	    public static int GetCellLength(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    29	    {
    30	#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    31	        return GetCellLengthRunes(text);
    32	#else
    33	        var sum = 0;
    34	        for (var i = 0; i < text.Length; i++)
    35	        {
    36	            var rune = text[i];
    37	            if (char.IsHighSurrogate(rune) && i + 1 < text.Length && char.IsLowSurrogate(text[i + 1]))
    38	            {
    39	                // Surrogate pair - calculate the actual code point
    40	                var codePoint = char.ConvertToUtf32(rune, text[i + 1]);
    41	                sum += UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(codePoint);
    42	                i++; // skip the low surrogate
    43	            }
    44	            else
    45	            {
    46	                sum += GetCellLength(rune);
    47	            }
    48	        }
    49	
    50	        return sum;
    51	#endif
    52	    }
    53	
    54	#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
    55	    private static int GetCellLengthRunes(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
    56	    {
    57	        // We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 sequences properly.
    58	        // We iterate through the span, decoding runes as we go,
    59	        // and maintain state for ZWJ/VS16 sequences.
    60	
    61	        var width = 0;
    62	        var i = 0;
    63	        Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;
    64	
    65	        while (i < text.Length)
    66	        {
    67	            System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out var rune, out var charsConsumed);
    68	            i += charsConsumed;
    69	
    70	            // Zero Width Joiner: skip this and the next rune
    71	            if (rune.Value == '\u200D')
    72	            {
    73	                // Skip the next rune
    74	                if (i < text.Length)
    75	                {
    76	                    System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out _, out var skipChars);
    77	                    i += skipChars;
    78	                }
    79	
    80	                continue;
    81	            }
    82	
    83	            // Variation Selector 16: conditionally add width
    84	            if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)
    85	            {
    86	                width += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
    87	                lastMeasureRune = null;
    88	                continue;
    89	            }
    90	
    91	            var wcw = GetCellLength(rune);
    92	            if (wcw > 0)
    93	            {
    94	                lastMeasureRune = rune;
    95	            }
    96	
    97	            width += wcw;
    98	        }
    99	
   100	        return width;
   101	    }
   102	#endif
   103	
   104	#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
   105	    public static int GetCellLength(Rune rune)
   106	    {
   107	        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
   108	        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
   109	        // calculate the size for new line characters.
   110	        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
   111	        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
   112	        // to work with string length and not cell length.
   113	        if (rune.Value == '\n')
   114	        {
   115	            return 1;
   116	        }
   117	
   118	        return UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
   119	    }
   120	#endif
   121	
   122	    public static int GetCellLength(char rune)
   123	    {
   124	        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
   125	        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
   126	        // calculate the size for new line characters.
   127	        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
   128	        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
   129	        // to work with string length and not cell length.
   130	        if (rune == '\n')
   131	        {
   132	            return 1;
   133	        }
   134	
   135	        // Surrogates should not be measured individually;
   136	        // they are part of a surrogate pair and should be handled
   137	        // via the Rune-based or string-based overloads.
   138	        if (char.IsSurrogate(rune))
   139	        {
   140	            return 0;
   141	        }
   142	
   143	        var width = _runeWidthCache[rune];
   144	        if (width == Sentinel)
   145	        {
   146	            width = (sbyte)UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
   147	            _runeWidthCache[rune] = width;
   148	        }
   149	
   150	        return width;
   151	    }
   152	}

+#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
+    private static int GetCellLengthRunes(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)
+    {
+        // We need to handle ZWJ and VS16 sequences properly.
+        // We iterate through the span, decoding runes as we go,
+        // and maintain state for ZWJ/VS16 sequences.
+
+        var width = 0;
+        var i = 0;
+        Rune? lastMeasureRune = null;
+
+        while (i < text.Length)
+        {
+            System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out var rune, out var charsConsumed);
+            i += charsConsumed;
+
+            // Zero Width Joiner: skip this and the next rune
+            if (rune.Value == '\u200D')
+            {
+                // Skip the next rune
+                if (i < text.Length)
+                {
+                    System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(text.Slice(i), out _, out var skipChars);
+                    i += skipChars;
+                }
+
+                continue;
+            }
+
+            // Variation Selector 16: conditionally add width
+            if (rune.Value == '\uFE0F' && lastMeasureRune != null)
+            {
+                width += Vs16Table.GetTable(Unicode.Version_9_0_0).Find(lastMeasureRune.Value.Value);
+                lastMeasureRune = null;
+                continue;
+            }
+
+            var wcw = GetCellLength(rune);
+            if (wcw > 0)
+            {
+                lastMeasureRune = rune;
+            }
+
+            width += wcw;
+        }
+
+        return width;
+    }
+#endif
+
+#if NET6_0_OR_GREATER
+    public static int GetCellLength(Rune rune)
+    {
+        // TODO: We need to figure out why Segment.SplitLines fails
+        // if we let wcwidth (which returns -1 instead of 1)
+        // calculate the size for new line characters.
+        // That is correct from a Unicode perspective, but the
+        // algorithm was written before wcwidth was added and used
+        // to work with string length and not cell length.
+        if (rune.Value == '\n')
+        {
+            return 1;
+        }
+
+        return UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(rune);
     }
+#endif
 
     public static int GetCellLength(char rune)
     {
@@ -49,6 +132,14 @@ internal static class Cell

         }

+        // via the Rune-based or string-based overloads.
+        if (char.IsSurrogate(rune))
+        {
